title: ‘Nine amazing years’: Jayson Tatum on his relationship with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum discussed his nine-year partnership with Jaylen Brown, citing mutual respect despite public speculation.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and draft picks, shocking Tatum.
Brad Stevens stated Tatum had no input on the trade, influenced by NBA's new financial rules.

title: 4 standout moments from Tom Brady’s appearance on New Heights
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady discusses his 'welcome to the NFL moment' involving a big hit from Buffalo's Nate Clements.
Brady highlights a Randy Moss catch in 2007 that signaled a special season for the Patriots.
Brady calls the Belichick versus Brady debate 'dumb,' emphasizing teamwork for the Patriots' success.

title: France vs. Morocco World Cup tickets: Here’s how much last-minute seats cost at Boston Stadium
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: France and Morocco will play a World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, Foxborough, at 4 p.m. on July 9, 2026.
Ticket prices for the match range from $2,182.48 to $3,290 depending on the vendor and fees included.
France is ranked No. 1 and Morocco is ranked No. 6 in the latest FIFA rankings for the tournament.

title: Brothers, town employees, allegedly attacked homeless man in Holbrook and broke several of his bones
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brothers Jesse, 32, and Jake Baxter, 28, accused of assaulting a homeless man in Holbrook on June 13.
The attack near Brookville Park left the victim with a concussion, broken bones, and multiple cuts.
Police linked Jesse Baxter's car to the scene, suspecting a mistaken identity during the alleged assault.

title: ‘It’s about my life’: Why acclaimed Cambridge wine bar Dear Annie is closing
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Dear Annie wine bar in Cambridge will end regular service on July 31, prioritizing private events starting September.
Owner Lauren Friel cites personal life priorities and motherhood as reasons for shifting the business focus of Dear Annie.
Dear Annie gained national fame, listed among The New York Times' and Bon Appétit's top U.S. restaurant picks in 2022.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ 2026 Summer League roster and schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' Summer League team includes second-year player Hugo Gonzalez and 2026 first-round draft pick Chris Cenac Jr.
Boston's Summer League schedule features games against the Raptors, Hornets, Hawks, and Kings at Cox Pavilion.
The Celtics' Summer League coach is Amile Jefferson; the team consists of both NBA and NCAA players.

title: Tartan Army makes a big donation to help Mass. students in need
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Tartan Army donated $15,000 to the Massachusetts Child charity for students experiencing economic hardship.
The funding was saved by using school buses for travel during Scotland's World Cup matches in Foxborough.
The donation will help local students purchase clothing, books, and school supplies, according to the Massachusetts Teachers Association.

title: Your neighborhood Cumbies could soon look a little different
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cumberland Farms' parent, EG Group, plans a public offering to fund store renovations and nationwide expansion.
Cumberland aims to refresh 60 locations annually in the Northeast and rebrand 600-700 sites nationwide over five years.
Cumberland reported a net loss of $145 million on $16.34 billion in revenue, highlighting expansion efforts to improve performance.

title: Boston sues social media companies over ‘addictive’ features, joining nationwide litigation
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston sues Meta, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube over addictive features targeting minors.
Lawsuit filed in California federal court will join over 1,500 similar complaints from US school districts.
Boston Public Schools report increased mental health issues in students, attributing it to social media use.

title: Cyclospora, the explosive diarrhea parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts reports 18 cyclosporiasis cases amid a nationwide total of 145 since May 1, according to the CDC.
Cyclosporiasis is linked to contaminated produce and can cause prolonged diarrhea, cramping, nausea, treatable with antibiotics.
No cluster of cases found in Massachusetts; past outbreaks linked to produce like raspberries, basil, and bagged salads.
